change: you change the F() formula (BFT fault-tolerance count = (N-1)/3)

impact.why: QuorumSize = ceil(N - F()); directly computed from F(). Changing F shifts the quorum threshold for the whole protocol.
Uses c.valSet.F() directly for the F+1 ROUND-CHANGE early-start rule; the only non-QuorumSize direct caller of F().
Calls QuorumSize() to decide when >= quorum COMMITs are reached to commit the block.
Calls QuorumSize() to transition to StatePrepared once quorum PREPAREs collected.
Passes QuorumSize() into isJustified() for round-change justification check.
Uses QuorumSize() both for the quorum-of-ROUND-CHANGE proposer trigger and inside isJustified().
Rejects seals with len(sealers) < QuorumSize(); seal verification threshold depends on F via QuorumSize.
Calls verifyAggregatedSeal for prev prepared/committed seals; transitively gated by QuorumSize.
Calls verifyAggregatedSeal for current prepared/committed seals; transitively gated by QuorumSize.
Dispatches to handlePrepare/handleCommit/handleRoundChange/handlePreprepare, all of which gate on QuorumSize/F.

grep_would_miss: The bare token 'F' is catastrophically noisy: grep 'F' matches every identifier containing F. Even grep '.F()' is drowned by unrelated *.F() method calls across the geth tree.

A naive grep on 'F' would MISS the real semantic dependency: QuorumSize() does not mention 'F' as a word boundary a human scans for; the dependency is the expression `valSet.F()` inside QuorumSize at line 228.
grep on 'F' would DROWN the only two genuine F() call-sites (roundchange.go F+1 logic) among thousands of false hits.
Transitive impact through QuorumSize (commit/prepare/preprepare/roundchange/verifyAggregatedSeal/verifyHeader) is invisible to any grep on 'F' — those sites reference QuorumSize, never F.
The interface contract types.go:240 F() float64 / 242 QuorumSize() int couples the two; grep on 'F' alone would not surface QuorumSize as the load-bearing dependent.
notes: Seed F() at default.go:222 excluded. QuorumSize is the central amplifier: nearly all impact flows F -> QuorumSize -> consensus handlers + seal verification. Only roundchange.go uses F() directly (F+1 rule). Interface decls in types.go (240/242) define the contract but are not 'updated' sites. _test.go (default_test.go) excluded. The correct way to find this is impact_analysis on QuorumSize, not grep on F.
